source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt:
Design a bolted multi-body aluminum joint assembly consisting of: (A) one 7075-T6 parent housing block (60 x 60 x 40 mm thick) machined to receive a single M10 x 1.5 tapped hole; (B) one wire-thread insert (M10 x 1.5 x 1.5D length = 15 mm, 18-8 stainless) installed into the STI M10 x 1.5 tapped hole; (C) one grade 8.8 M10 hex-head bolt that mates into the insert during service.

The tapped hole is centered on the 60 x 60 face, drilled to the standard STI tap drill size 10.3 mm diameter to an effective depth of 19 mm, then tapped STI M10 x 1.5 and a 1.5D (15 mm) insert installed. The parent block is 7075-T6 aluminum (yield 503 MPa, UTS 572 MPa, Young's modulus 71.7 GPa, density 2810 kg/m^3). Nominal grade 8.8 M10 bolt proof load 47.3 kN, nominal UTS-based tensile capacity 58.0 kN (catalog grade 8.8 M10).

Load cases: LC1 pure axial tension 47.3 kN (bolt proof load, which is the wire-thread-insert rated service condition for 1.5D length in parent UTS >= 400 MPa per wire-thread-insert design manual); LC2 axial tension 58.0 kN (bolt UTS) as the ultimate check; LC3 combined axial 30 kN + transverse shear 10 kN.

Pass/fail on the assembled model: (R1) at LC1 proof load, the thread-engagement shear in the aluminum parent at the insert OD <= 0.577 * 503 / 1.2 = 242 MPa (SF 1.2 on aluminum shear-yield, wire-thread-insert design manual); (R2) at LC2 ultimate load, parent thread shear <= 0.577 * 572 = 330 MPa (no stripping before bolt fractures); (R3) the insert's own thread engagement length 1.5D provides an 18-8 stainless shear capacity per catalog >= bolt UTS load (insert cannot be the weak link); (R4) block max von Mises under LC3 <= 503 / 1.5 = 335 MPa (SF 1.5 on yield for general structural aluminum); (R5) bolt bearing stress under the head on the aluminum surface <= 0.9 * yield = 453 MPa (bearing check per VDI 2230); (R6) edge distance from insert OD to any free edge of the block >= 2 x insert major diameter = 21 mm (wire-thread-insert edge-distance chart for adjacent-hole / edge-distance in 7075); (R_asm1) assembly-scope connection DCR on the insert-parent thread-shear interface and the insert-bolt thread-shear interface <= 1.0 under LC1 and LC2.

Manufacturing: CNC mill block; drill and STI-tap per standard wire-thread-insert installation instruction; install insert with prewinder. The final FEM check runs on the fully assembled model with three distinct bodies (parent block + wire-thread insert + bolt) and thread-pressure contact at both the insert-parent and insert-bolt interfaces. Per-part stress is reported but assembly-level passing requires all interface DCRs and thread-shear limits to hold simultaneously.

Deliverables: STEP AP242 of the assembled joint with the block, insert, and bolt as distinct bodies tagged with part_family and instance_index; report with parent thread shear stress at LC1 and LC2, block max von Mises at LC3, head bearing stress, compliance with wire-thread-insert minimum edge distance, per-part max stress, and assembly-level connection DCR.
short_prompt:
Design a three-body aluminum tapped-insert joint delivered as a multi-part assembly: a 7075-T6 parent block (60 x 60 x 40 mm, yield 503 MPa, UTS 572 MPa, E = 71.7 GPa, rho = 2810 kg/m^3), an 18-8 stainless M10 x 1.5 x 1.5D (15 mm length) wire-thread insert installed into an STI-tapped hole (tap drill 10.3 mm, depth 19 mm), and a grade 8.8 M10 hex-head bolt. Edge distance from insert OD to any free edge >= 21 mm (2 x insert major diameter per wire-thread-insert edge-distance chart).

Load cases.
- LC1 proof tension: 47.3 kN (grade 8.8 M10 proof).
- LC2 ultimate tension: 58.0 kN (grade 8.8 M10 UTS).
- LC3 combined service: 30 kN axial + 10 kN transverse shear.

Governing reference: wire-thread-insert design manual + VDI 2230 Part 1 + MIL-I-8846. Pass limits: parent thread shear at insert OD <= 0.577 * 503/1.2 = 242 MPa at LC1 and <= 0.577 * 572 = 330 MPa at LC2; block max von Mises at LC3 <= 503/1.5 = 335 MPa; head bearing stress <= 0.9 * 503 = 453 MPa.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: VDI 2230 preload, thread shear area, and engagement length formulas are closed-form.

prompt.title: 7075-T6 block with M10 x 1.5 wire-thread insert rated for grade 8.8 bolt (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: Three-body tapped-insert joint: 7075-T6 parent block + M10 x 1.5 x 1.5D wire-thread insert + grade 8.8 M10 bolt. Must withstand bolt proof (47.3 kN) and ultimate (58.0 kN) without parent or insert stripping. Final FEM check runs on the assembled model.

parts.name: aluminum_parent_block
parts.role: 7075-T6 parent housing that receives the wire-thread insert in an STI-tapped hole

parts.name: wire_thread_insert
parts.role: 1.5D length wire-thread insert (MS122XX-equivalent) installed into the STI-tapped hole

parts.name: hex_head_bolt
parts.role: grade 8.8 M10 hex-head bolt that engages the insert during service

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: 60 x 60 x 40 mm (parent block) plus bolt head extending above the top face
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: Tapped hole centered on the 60 x 60 top face; bolt axis along Z

boundary_conditions.block_base: fully fixed at the face opposite the tapped hole
boundary_conditions.bolt_interface: bolt modeled with head bearing + thread engagement pressure
boundary_conditions.inter_part_fastener_stiffness: thread-pressure contact at insert-parent and insert-bolt interfaces with friction per VDI 2230

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Wire-thread-insert design manual: 1.5D length in 18-8 stainless >= mating bolt UTS
2 x insert major dia 10.5 per wire-thread-insert edge-distance chart
assembly-scope DCR on insert-parent and insert-bolt thread-shear interfaces

requirements.governing_standard: Wire-thread-insert design manual + VDI 2230 Part 1 + MIL-I-8846 (for 1.5D length in aluminum)

analysis_types: linear static with bolt-preload + thread pressure

interfaces.load_path: insert external thread engages parent STI thread; pull-out resisted by thread-shear in aluminum
bolt external thread engages insert internal thread; transmits axial + shear service load

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: A tapped wire-thread insert joint is fundamentally a three-body system (parent + insert + bolt) whose pull-out capacity depends on two distinct thread-shear interfaces with different material pairs.
notes.speculation_caveats: All three body counts (1 each) are dictated by the joint topology; the bolt body is included explicitly to preserve the service-load path.
